comment_content:
Whilst the drama's premise is beautiful, the comments are disheartening and disappointing. We're in 2025 and yet people are still whining about there's not enough black this or that out there. Spike Lee overcame immense challenges to make She's Gotta Have It (a story incidentally about black love), and similarly black film makers before him. Now technology is so pervasive and accessible, you have no excuse but to go out and make you're own stories if you don't see yourself on screen.

Some candidates from the last 30 or so years.

Awkward Black Girl

Entergalactic

Poetic Justice

Love Jones (1997)

Love & Basketball (2000)

If Beale Street Could Talk (2018) 

Rye Lane (2023) - 

Premature (2019)

ATL 2006

The Wood (1999)

Dope (2015)

She's Gotta Have It

A Love Song for Latasha

My Fault 2025

Sylvie's Love 2020

I don't know all those listed, but some favs Entergalactic and Love and Basketball. Although before my time. Knew it from my big cousins. Plus all the ton of good quality indie dramas on YouTube and African and UK dramas. There's more than enough out there not to warrant that type of scarcity mindset shown in the comments.
